---

### 4. `syntropia/en/index.qmd` (Modelo de Syntropía en Inglés)

```markdown
---
title: "Syntropia — Clinical and Scientific Model"
format: html
---

<!-- Language Switcher Header -->
<div class="absolute top-6 right-8 z-50 text-sm md:text-base font-medium text-white tracking-wide">
  <p>
    <span class="font-bold border-b-2 border-teal-400 pb-0.5">EN</span>
    <span class="mx-2 text-slate-400">|</span> 
    <a href="../es/" class="text-slate-300 hover:text-white hover:underline transition-all">ES</a>
  </p>
</div>

```{=html}
<!-- Hero -->
<section class="hero-bg min-h-screen flex items-center relative overflow-hidden text-white">
  <div class="max-w-5xl mx-auto px-6 text-center">
    <h1 class="text-6xl md:text-7xl font-bold leading-tight mb-6">
      Syntropia
    </h1>
    <p class="text-2xl md:text-3xl text-slate-200 mb-10 max-w-3xl mx-auto">
      The operational formalization of process ontology applied to clinical and scientific practice.
    </p>
    <div class="flex flex-col sm:flex-row gap-4 justify-center">
      <a href="/en/" class="border border-slate-300 hover:border-white px-10 py-5 rounded-2xl text-lg transition-all">Back to Rhysis</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- Model Content -->
<section class="py-24 bg-white">
  <div class="max-w-4xl mx-auto px-6">
    <h2 class="text-4xl font-bold text-center mb-12 text-slate-800">The Core of the Model</h2>
    <div class="text-slate-600 text-lg leading-relaxed space-y-7">
      <p>Syntropia organizes evaluation and intervention based on the integration of dynamic flows and successive individuations, leaving behind closed diagnostics built on fixed essences or systems.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- Footer -->
<footer class="py-16 bg-slate-900 text-slate-400 text-center">
  <p class="mb-2">© 2026 Syntropia — Clinical and Scientific Model</p>
</footer>
